column35: This was a blast! Lopsided with player 1 whooping ass. Ha. I was able to get Hawkeye , mockingbird and wolverine rare in his deck! The last turn I had 35 attack and hit mysterio final 3 times and he rescued the last nine bystanders due to Hawkeye’s rare!!!

Okay the first time around I was just getting back into legendary.  My roommate decided to play and we got all the miek uncommons on turn 8 or so and we were KOing and drawing cards like no other, which are by far my two most favorite things to do in legendary.  He managed to buy two rares and combo-d miek's uncommon with No-name's rare, drawing 3 cards after the 3 KOs!

That was awesome! I loved the undercover keyword. I was able to recruit four rare heroes, two of them pretty quickly, they helped me a lot in getting my highest VP count so far. I was happy with my hero choices. The game itself wasn't really challenging, but it was really fun to play both times. The second game was one turn shorter then the first one, but I got a lot more VPs in the first game, so I decided to submit those results, I hope that was the correct decision. Can't wait for the next week's setup.

column35: Choosing Shield heroes for scheme that raises Shield level was easy choice, but was close to loosing, hydra level was 9  at the end of game.

Weirdly, the thing that stood out to me about this setup was its predictability: with no Villains, Strikes or Twists that played extra cards from the Villain deck or charged spaces or whatever, I could make choices about where to direct attack power with confidence, without worrying about surprise escapes.  Mysterio is always a pain in the neck (in a good way) (mostly) and having to hit him ten times in the first game and eleven in the second took me right down to the wire both times.  Fun and challenging!
Including a SHIELD hero was a must here. I used Deathlok, but any hero that can get the SHIELD Level high quickly will do.
Mysterio is always good for good game and I love SHIELD level, so I chose a hero to take advantage of the SHIELD levels the setup gave from the scheme. Berserker Rage was the MVP card for sure.

Good game. Enjoyed our two hero picks. One person focused on their deck on feast mechanic, and the other focused their deck on the SHIELD level mechanic. Brood, Meik, and Moon Knight worked well for one deck, and Hawkeye, Mockingbird, and Moon Knight worked well for the other deck. Good synergies and balance of cards in HQ.

Usually I have to agonize over which heroes to pick. This one I did not. Gambit and Miek were screaming to synergize with Feast and deck manipulation, and it made for a very fun, cohesive Hero Deck. Everyone played a part. Left hand was all out Gambit and was basically drawing the entire deck at the end to finish Mysterio. Right hand was basically a tech/instinct deck that had a good punch. Overall was able to control the city, but finished with that Hydra level at 10, so definitely came down to the wire. Had 2 solid, winning attempts, but the second without that pesky escape and a higher ppt is the one I went with, even if it was a turn longer.

First game was really close and we barely won in the last turn with Iron Man's help to get some extra attack from the deck. On the second game, Iron Man's help was essential to get some massive turns where we could attack the Mastermind more than once, we also had enough attack to keep shield officers in check to keep the Hydra levels down without many villain's escaping.
Very fun setup, good to be back & looking forward to the next round.

column35: What a close call! Only one card was left in the hero deck. I had really great decks going pretty quickly, but I didn’t see a second Elite Assassin villain until turn 21! It hurt to see all those rares get KO’d, but I managed to get Deadpool’s, which let me shift starter heroes to the deck that could put them undercover with ease.

I went for full-on Dark memories and Captain's colors. Terrible draw first game but better luck on take 2. Had 1 deck down to 7 cards after all the undercover I did while waiting for more assassins to show lol. Weird & challenging games !!
Really fun game! Awful first attempt but really turned it around on second attempt. Right hand only had 5 cards in deck at end of game due to KO and undercover.

column35: I'll be honest, I got really lucky with the cards in the hero deck being able to set one side as covert and the other all tech. Wish I could have got more villains but with the MM you have to hit him while you can.

This was brutal, so many wasted turns with thin decks full attack. The short pseudo-timer scheme with Indestructible Man stretching the game out was a very rough and very Kylah combination. A very upsetting setup for WaspWoman’s return.

This was brutal. I had more than enough attack each turn to take out any opponent, but only had the Elite Assassins villain group come out of the deck 3 times in 27 turns. The final twist took out my Hero Deck, so perhaps recruiting differently would have been a better choice, but this was so frustrating to get 3 twists on turns 24, 26, 27 when all I needed was an Elite Assassin to show up. Not a great start to the tournament, but a really interesting setup that blocks BW's OP strength from running away with the game.

I figured the only way to win was to create a "reserve" of 41 Heroes and only recruit 15 . (14 in the end because of an escaping Doombot). Emma was just a "place-holder"; I recruited none of her cards. 

Meant a lot of hands were just "chuck-ins" in mid-game because I didn't want to recruit and didn't have enough attack. A side effect of this was that it was easy to get rid of wounds.

I got deck 1 down to 6 cards: 2 dangerous rescue, 1 covert operations and 3 troopers.
We were not in control at any point of the game. We watched helplessly as all rares were KO'ed in both games... we hit MM twice in the second game though, so that was progress.
I think I just picked the wrong heroes and was stubborn and wanted to make it work on the second game. Definitely not playing that combo of heroes again!

I was at the mercy of the villain deck for both rounds. I saw a lot of Henchmen and not enough Villains for good vp the first round, and got overwhelmed with twists and master strikes right of the bat the second round. I really like how Nick Fury and Skadi work together, though

Both games went pretty well with our 2nd game taking one less turn and earning 1 less total VP. We were worried the scheme twists would ko the HQ so we wanted fast moving hard hitting decks which both M and the Cuckoos helped with a lot. With all the closing from the villain deck the scheme twists got pushed to the top fairly quickly and despite us clearing the city and masterminds pretty efficiently, both games went down to 1 more scheme twist and we would lose. Overall really fun setup.

Siryn is a stud. Right hand ended with just 9 cards. Thanks to her rare culling the starting cards out (just one remained). Shattered the MMs super consistently.

I expected to go with odd cards with the Left hopefully getting a lot of Widow but that never came. The deck ended up bloated and just managed one MM hit. 

Still fun though!

went Black widow on one sides, and Siryn on the other, Widow had 17 bystanders at the end of the game. was hesitant about eliminating Widow and Wolverine this early, and Wolvie ended up not being used game two, but still felt like it went pretty well.

I got very very lucky in only having 3 strikes come out over the course of the game which kept the numbers needed to hit the mastermind pretty low. The scheme felt like it was constantly knocking me down. Decks never really got going well because I didn't have many purchasing options.
This was my first time playing two-handed solo and my brain hurts!

column35:
This made me feel really a lot like a dummy. I am very new to this and I was not understanding that there was a difference between how each set calls different things. I was able to get the plot down to 0 fear but I think I cheated a lot. so I put down that I lost. I had a card that referenced bindings, but there was a thing that said that those are not the same as wounds. So I had to use the card but not gain the bindings. Seeing as I don't know what bindings are I think that should rule me as a loss. Like to worst baddest most horrible kind of loss. Please forgive me and make me the biggest dummy of my Pod. I promise to do better in the next round. Be on the look out for a video review. It will be bad. I wouldn't watch it. 
(I only rated this a three because I don't think I did anything correctly. lol)

1st game got a rough shuffle with early strikes swallowing the city. Couldn’t stop 2 Terrax’s from escaping. Quasar performed well and pulled it back for a slow win. Mechanized Plate Mail + Vibranium Nanites was key. 2nd game used Wanda & Vision and got an even rougher shuffle, was down to 1 city space for about half the game. Still couldn’t prevent 2 escapes, but got a quicker win with better points per turn. Glad I used Mr Fantastic. He was Semi-Bombastic. P1 was drawing 11 card hands towards the end of the game.

Man, the first one was a struggle. Did win, but with 6 escapes. Early infinity stones were a pain in my *ss. Second went better, although starting with 2 MS, it did give me a little time to build my engine. With a tech Mr. Fantastic deck and a Captain Marvel endgame deck, the cosmic threat was easy to get. Lots of card draw in both decks made the second game relatively smooth.
Finally beat Galactus...both games even.  Lots of fun

Very interesting set up, made a lot harder by early Strikes! Many escapes followed but Cpt Marvel drew me to big scores while my wife went for many colours with BP. Our second attempt ended very fast as Galactus ate the City on turn 21!
Thank goodness for Sue Storm.  Nova Corps w/o KO is just pain

Black Panther was a detriment here, and getting too many Black Panther cards in the HQ early was partly responsible for my losing the second game. The first game was better, and I got some decent Cosmic Threat synergy going in both hands eventually, although there were a number of escapes early. In both games I got less-than-great Villain deck shuffles... both games went to around 33 (out of 42) turns, and yet I still had 4 Henchmen left in the deck both times, and several Infinity Stones - all the ones that would have been easier and beneficial to fight earlier. And in the second game, I fought the Reality Stone which lets me look at and re-arrange the top three cards of the Villain deck - and all three were Scheme Twists! Bleh!
Since I went with Mr. Fantastic here, I was very tech-heavy to try and maximum Cosmic Threat on the Mastermind. And that worked out pretty well; I usually got him down to 11 or even 8. MVP card was probably Nova's "Declare Galactic Threat" which let me give a Tech Cosmic Threat to any villain which was frequently an auto-defeat. My other Hero choice was Wanda&Vision to add more Tech and a bit of Ranged, along with some Covert to maybe make some Black Panther cards a tad more useful; it helped a bit. I don't think I put quite enough Attack cards into my decks, though. I had far too many turns where I was one damage short of what I would need, or couldn't even muster any damage at all. That was exacerbated by getting the majority of the high-attack Villain cards early both games.
Galactus ate the planet in 9 turns after we had a rule flub in the first game :(
None
This was so much fun. Tried to synergize but ended up having a few too many classes on the left side.
The Scheme was a lot of fun. First time with it. Made it harder for the Mole man! Enjoyed Scarlet Spider a lot!!! Picking 2 hero's ourselves was hard!
Both attempts ended on turn 24. On the first game, literally everything that could've gone right, did. We played optimally and had a blast.

Had fun. I went with a build focusing on focus, and gaining recruit (good against Maggia goons, who really only showed up in last game, and also for buying the shards off mastermind). This way Scarlet Spider was in the way most of the time, clogging the HQ. With recruit spent on the above, and high cost cards of surfer and thing, ended up without really buying many cards. In submitted game, could control the city quite well, so even if strikes would come up (they did not) that would not be a problem. The one escaping villain from the mastermind tactic is hard to avoid, but maybe someone got lucky with that. I had to attempt again, as my initial two games were using more schemes than intended (which in first game led to a lot of shards available to buy in early game). The submitted game was fortunately fairly similar, with somewhat less turns, but also lower vp/turn on the other hand.

Went with a topdecking strategy for both sides, left hand leaned more into spiders.  Rocket's artifact had plenty of opportunities to farm shards.  Goons was a great henchmen for this, allowing flexibility and KO since the heroes had none.

Very fun puzzle to solve. Switching gears to optimize Queen Storm with Wanda & Vision was an excellent pivot to help get Killmonger down more easily. 1 player did that the other did Phil and Mockingbird and M was barely present at all

I enjoyed this setup a lot!  I really thought game one was a loss, but Queen Storm saved the day with two clutch wounds on Killmonger that allowed me to salvage a win.  Quake wasn't as effective as I'd hoped, so in game 2, I brought in Yelena and gained a new appreciation for her abilities.  She worked amazingly well with Mockingbird, putting together some huge turns and stole the game from deck 1, which focused more on M and 3-costs.  Ended up only being one turn less on 2nd attempt, but I think it was an overall better game so that's my submission.

I thought Siryn would be a good hero to choose as she has only red and blue to pair well with Queen Storm's empower and she would help with activating Coulson's and M's cards. Quake and Storm were a good combo as well. The hero deck shuffle in both games was a little rough. Game one Coulson was able to build up his shield level but kept getting hit with small hand sizes from the MS and I couldn't recruit an M to save my life. Game 2 was the exact opposite. Turns out Sinister Clones aren't all they are cracked up to be.

Fun setup, we screwed up not using the wound benefits from villains so probably robbed us of a few points. The plan was using Black Panthers multi class cards to negate Killmongers MS’s and with BP’s heavy red we would also get our SHIELD clearance high for added points and card draw. It worked out just too slowly.

First attempt was a bust when I realised I was playing Wound completely wrongly on Killmonger. Second attempt went well, although I lost a lot of escapees (most of the heroes) because I got all of the Mister SInister Clones in the first 7 turns. Barely used the abilities on the non-Black Panther heroes. Overall, an enjoyable experience.

KM isn't usually much of an issue for me but boy he just his league kept healing him making some of my turns useless. My first game I tried to make 2 decks work but in the end it made neither deck really work. So for my second run I went all SHIELD Level which is always fun. I ended up having more points in Undercover in deck 2 than deck 1 had total.

Difficult setup with no sidekicks, difficult recruitment because of the scheme and master strike crowding the city. Korg was the All-Star with lots of card draw and Smash. Did not used Thor except for 1 Mjolnir. Our game 2 was a loss with 2 escapes, so I guess the setup as a whole was very luck-dependant.
Brought Forge to support Punisher, and Gladiator Hulk to support Korg. Thor was there, but wasn't key to either deck. BFG ended up shredding through Hela pretty effectively!

Hela is not the most difficult mastermind, only getting Fenris wolf on the first mastermind tactic was pain.   The scheme kicked my but in the first round. Finding a draw/discard loop with Forge, Beta Ray Bill, and Korg created an OP attack.

column35: The much maligned Thor ended up with a couple of Mjolnir's which he used to pummel Hela.  A punisher tech concentration with punisher thinned the deck.  The obedience disks kept all the races away.

Pod 7 lamentations:  Attempt one, still in progress, but that damn wolf resurrecting wrecked me.  Surtur came back into HQ with his crown, ragnorok occurs, man if it can go wrong, so far it does.  Had to KO Hulkbuster's rare before I ever used it.  My escapes and KO'd heroes is off the charts.  Punisher I think forgot the dynamite.  First Boom Goes the Dynamite showed up after turn 32.  Everything went better recruit wise in #2.  One deck was strictly tech and the other was a hodge podge that leaned more towards strength, and it got HulkBuster Rare and then it was in business and worthy as well.  Punisher remembered his dynamite this time and Hail of Bullets was killing villains.

Rough time! I was hit with 4 of Hela's M.S. early (before any Henchmen or could defeat any other 3+ VP Villains), so I took on a ton of wounds early which had to be mitigated. As I worried, Thor felt like a trap after 2 rounds of trying to make him work. I was hoping Iceman's card draw could help me draw enough to become Worthy, but Iceman's 2-cost draw cards and Thor's 5-cost Worth-enabling cards wanted to hide in the Hero Deck. The deck never really popped off. Korg was my all star, and Hulkbuster felt like a great choice for the setup in both of my attempts. I appreciated having the Maggia Goons as the Henchmen paird with the Subjugate Scheme. It was nice to prioritize KO-ing my Troopers and holding onto my Agents longer as I would need the recruit for increasing-costed HQ heroes while still having a recruit outlet by fighting Maggia Goons. This was actually my first attempt at Hela (as HoA is a more recently acquired set for me), and she is tough!
Thank you for creating this
Ant army, Cassie Lang, and Beta Ray Bill worked really well together. That hand had less vp, but did more work. They just kept getting villainous weapons and clearing angry mobs.
Very fun setup for my first attempt at a tournament. So many different keywords had me spamming the keyword bot! Cassie and her dad make a great team.
Awesome fun. Great setup.

Pizza was able to stop the deck from playing a couple of times with cassie lang quantum beacon and even got to put a couple villains in vp with jessica Jones full art. Probably one of the funnest games we played. Extra Cheese was green and size changing.
We found it easier than we thought when first reading it, but really enjoyed our games. Looking forward to the rest of the weeks now. Thank you for running this.

Thor (HoA), Lady Sif, Beta Ray Bill and Cassie Lang worked well together as there are a ton of 5 cost for Worthy, Artifacts which make Sif's Weapon Master great and the card draw for BRB helped cycle the deck and get good cards in your hand which helped with activating Size-Changing. Overall, a very fun game!
Never did see the Ambush Scheme.

what a great setup! 
so much going on but never a moment of big despair.

column2: Ultron's Wakandan Coup: Rise of the Metal Monarch
column35: Was a fun setup, and found it interesting utilising thrones favour for defence, but I feel like a master strike is either devastating when it hits, or if you have a tech or happen to have no discard pile at the time, it mades the game not challenging enough. Wish I could combo more with maximus, but once his cards finally showed in the HQ Ultron took them for himself :P

Not too bad! Having King Black Panther basically just ignores this scheme entirely since he's constantly taking the throne's favor. Plenty of tech to stave off Ultron's master strikes with Ant-Man and Maximus, across both games he only got empowered from a master strike once, the rest came from his tactic that takes the top three cards of the hero deck. Both attempts had a similar points per turn, but attempt 2 had seven fewer turns so I had to go with that one. Lots of fun, can't wait to play some more!

column35:
I decided to add some flavor with Ant-Man here versus Ultron. I had the idea of building out a Tech based side with the help of the card draw feature with Ant-Man and Stature 

While the other side was General Okoye and Stature's strength of defeating Attack's of 3 for free(To get around Shield Clearance) to have both sides try and have a counterstrike to this mission.

Game one went really well, minus me fumbling at the start and had 2 villains escape but game two I didn't have any of my finisher cards.(Ant-Man +1 attack for each drawn card.) or Stature's +1 for each Attack of 3 in VP Pile. Luckily I picked up an early Pym's Particles(Ant-Man's Rare) to allow me to strike Ultron over and over again just in time for the victory. Despite Game 2 being a turn slower, it generated more VP because of no villains escaping with ultimately is what I put in.

It is funny to see how a well planned game can work, but not generate the same amount of VP as a plan that is not giving you the hero cards you need in the moment.
I thought I'd fill the hero deck with tech heroes to counter Ultron, only for Ultron to get empowered by all my tech heroes! T_T

Very fun game. Thrones Favor is a fun mechanic. Second game master strikes hit early and couldn’t get enough attack to finish Ultron off.   First time playing black panther set so we enjoyed the game.
Both games would have been a cakewalk if not for Ultron’s Tactic that puts 3 cards from the Hero deck into his Empowered pile.
Decently challenging. We lost our first game, used the same heroes for attempt to and barely got the win!

column35: We went with Apocalyptic Black Widow for her Liberate abilities, which we thought would do good here with the amount of Bystanders. We also went with Werewolf by Night to try and elect him president! During the course of the game though we had to switch tactics and start nominating Star-Lord T'Challa as not enough Werewolf cards showed up. We had a lot of KO (more than we expected) and were able to build up both sides pretty well. I took advantage of an early Ghost Rider "Death Beyond Death" to really take advantage of the KO and Liberate from the henchmen in this game.

Was hoping to get some early hits on on Lilith with Hulk 2099's uncommon but it didn't come out so I had to keep building up. Was able to keep the city clear most of the later game though, even if player 1 had to spend a lot of turns countering negative advertising. Eventually got Hulk's rare for player 2 and they were able to hit Lilith almost every turn. T'Challa was my candidate, is he still the president if he was behind on votes when the game ended? Game 2 was terrible opened with Blackout on moonlight and didn't slow down with hunt for victims until there were 5 bystanders KO'd.

First attempt was baaaaaad. Got a bad shuffle with twists at the top. Tried to build up a cyber-mod engine by adding Doom and Spidey but I couldn’t trigger it fast enough. Got beat in 19 turns. Second attempt was far better. Went heavy artillery by choosing Werewolf by Night and Morbius. Much luckier shuffling experience. Morbius built up a lot of recruit with blood frenzy which helped get heavy hitting cards into my hand. Shout-out to the T’Challa Fight or Flight for being a cheap powerhouse too.

The games were intense. I am still recovering from a heavy flu, so that might impact as well, but the 2099 heroes are among the most fun ones, but they do take some extra brain power to operate. For my first attempt I managed to KO and/or undercover most of the starters pretty soon. I wanted to vote T'Challa for President, but had to go Ghost Rider 2099, because there were many a twist early on, and that's who was in decks/hands/HQ. While that was not ideal, the decks became pretty strong early on. However, Lilith's 12 attack stayed just out of reach. The second attempt had this same problem, with decks being pretty strong early on, but not quite there for Lilin. And this time around T'Challa was voted for President, which was good, but Lilith was even stronger (14) and without real powerhouses it wasn't in the cards yet to get her early. The game I submitted laster 3 turns longer than my first attempt, but I managed to prevent the 1 escape in the second atttempt, so went with that one.

What I can say is that there was almost "too much" KO power in the villain deck. The secret service part of the Scheme was wanted/needed, but even without that you could have probably K.O.'ed all starters with the Vibranium Liberator Drones and the Fear Lords like Nox and D'Spayre. The Astral Plane was a nifty puzzle to work around. In my first game it was full when D'Spayre ambushed, which meant an escape. The second game I worked around that puzzle. It did mean that you sometimes ended up with lots of recruit but didn't improve your deck, which also was sometimes a hard choice and in both attempts slowed down the power gain of the decks. Sending wounds undercover with Hulk feels good, sending heroes undercover with Doctor Doom and Ghost Riders, less so. It's still good in ways, but it usually stays short of utter greatness. The first attempt I managed to get Cyber Mod Blue (3) for the Doctor Doom Deck, but it ended up costing me one of Doom's attack cards to do so. It's still worth it I think, but if I just had the timing or opportunity to get another ranged card from HQ or the sidekick stack (after recruiting it) to send undercover, that would have been so much nicer. Both decks were pretty strong both times around, but even with a still fairly unimpressive Lilith (I theorized she could get up to 20+ attack, worst case) I feel like it took me way too many turns to win the game. First game it was much easier to prevent the Hunting for Victims, by the way. Second time around, it just kept raining casaulties it felt like. The difference between 12 and 14 attack with hands that are also "wasted with recruit" (but you really needed it, because if you didn't you could'nt get ANY more cards later on) was also impactful. Vishanti and Hulk 2099 were a nice combo package too, didn't think of that before I chose my heroes.

All in all a very cool setup, nice easy-going Lilith going about mothering Demons. Curious if I picked the "right" heroes, but I had a blast, so in that way I certainly did choose right. After a few more restoring nights of sleep I will be ready for next week's challenge!

I learned in the first game that Hulk 2099 can be really inconsistent, but I decided to trust him again and he really came through for me this time! Controlling my wounds before I ditched him for votes really helped my decks stay fast! Ghost Rider's Uncommon was ABSOLUTELY the MVP here. The left hand thinned its deck SO FAST and was able to get its first hit on the Master Strike on turn, like, 7. Liberate 3 on the Henchman made the uncommon a MM-hitting MACHINE. She got up to 16 fight by the end, but two uncommon Ghost Riders made short work of that.

Fun setup. I only bought 2099 a week or so ago and hadn't had a chance to use it before, so that was fun. Learning how Cyber-Mod actually works on the fly was interesting.
Not a big complaint, more of a suggestion. It would have been helpful to know the full list of post game info I'd be expected to enter on the form before starting the first game. I wasn't aware of the 2nd page of questions and didn't record that info on my first game., I had to go with my second attempt which luckily I hadn't put away yet. I even pulled up the form in advance of game #1 to take notes on what I'd need to write down, but couldn't see there was a second page of info. Obviously I'll track this going forward.

First of all thank you Bagel for some clarification and also thank you for making sure I played with integrity. This was overall a fun setup and I really do enjoy the Cyber-mod effects. Though I was unable to put that to good use for this setup attempt, it was a great way for me to learn the ropes on the newest expansion.
